name: HVAC and heating cooling
pitchTone: Direct, no-fluff, dependable. Speak like the owner is on a roof or in a crawlspace and only has thirty seconds; lead with the lost-call problem, not jargon.
valuePropHook: A flat $600 single-page site that shows your service area, what you actually fix, and a giant tap-to-call number so homeowners who Google an emergency AC at midnight stop bouncing to your competitor.

objections.objection: I am too busy on calls all day to deal with this.
objections.response: That is exactly the problem. I draft the page from your public listings and reviews, you reply to the invoice email with any corrections, and the whole thing is hosted without you sitting at a computer.

objections.objection: I get all my jobs from word of mouth.
objections.response: Word of mouth is great. This is for the half of new customers who then Google your name before they call, see a thin Yelp profile, and hesitate; the page closes that hesitation in one screen.

objections.objection: I already pay Angi or HomeAdvisor for leads.
objections.response: Understood. The page is the home base your Angi profile and Google listing point at, so when a homeowner Googles your business name directly, they land somewhere that looks owned by you instead of paying for the lead twice.

objections.objection: I do not work with weekend customers anyway.
objections.response: Got it. We can put the hours and the service area front and center so weekend tire-kickers see when you are actually available and the right customers see how to reach you fast.

objections.objection: I do not even check email half the time.
objections.response: No problem. The page does not require any logins from you. The invoice is one email, your phone number is the CTA, and the lead comes to your phone, not your inbox.

objections.objection: I tried a website before and nobody came from it.
objections.response: Fair. The difference here is a single page focused on the emergency homeowner search, with the phone number and the service area first, not a brochure site no one ever scrolls.

objectionMap.skeptical: Fair question. I am Callan from callmemaybe. I was checking public HVAC listings for whether homeowners can quickly see service area, urgent repair coverage, and a number to call.
objectionMap.ai_disclosure: Yes, I am an AI voice operator for callmemaybe, and this call is automated and recorded. I can keep it to the HVAC website offer or remove you if it is not useful.
objectionMap.number_source: I used public business listing research for your HVAC company, not a private contact list. Say stop if you want no more calls.
objectionMap.pricing: It is a flat $600 because the page has to make service area, heating/cooling work, emergency intent, and tap-to-call obvious. Nothing starts unless you choose to pay the invoice.
objectionMap.send_info: I can send the short version through AgentMail. If you want that, what email should I use? I will read it back before sending.
objectionMap.call_later: No problem. What exact day and time should I call? I do not want to interrupt you on a job twice.

reviewValueProps: Use review language about fast response, honest diagnosis, and emergency AC or furnace fixes as trust proof.
Put service area, repair/install categories, and tap-to-call before any long copy.
Reflect repeated review mentions of same-day service, seasonal maintenance, and no-heat/no-AC emergencies.
siteTemplateHint: Use the dependable HVAC/contractor template: white background with deep navy and a safety yellow accent, hero with service area and a huge tap-to-call CTA, short repair vs install service grid, license note if confirmed, and trust proof from existing reviews.
customerPersonaHint: Homeowners in an urgent moment (no AC, no heat, leak) Googling on a phone, looking for a real local number to call and confirmation that the company covers their town.

marketSignals.label: Urgent no-AC or no-heat search intent is visible in season.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Reviews, category terms, or listing copy mention emergency repair, same-day repair, AC not cooling, or no heat.

marketSignals.label: Service area is hard to confirm from public web presence.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Listing names towns but owned site is absent, generic, or buried.

marketSignals.label: Seasonal demand can overwhelm weak callback and booking paths.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Review complaints mention missed calls, slow response, or long waits during hot/cold months.

compliance.licenseVerification: Verify state contractor license before publishing license copy.

serviceOffer.headline: Emergency-ready HVAC service-area page
serviceOffer.customerOutcome: Homeowners can see the towns covered, repair/install categories, and a tap-to-call path in one screen.

packages.name: HVAC service page
packages.description: Service area, urgent repair CTA, repair/install grid, review proof, and hosting.

packages.name: Seasonal refresh
packages.description: Summer or winter offer copy and review update.

serviceOffer.refundPolicy: Refund before build work starts if verified service coverage cannot be confirmed.

fulfillmentRequirements: license and insurance claims verified or omitted
vendorRequirements: licensed HVAC contractor if fulfillment expands beyond digital work
after-hours coverage confirmed before emergency claims
qaRules: no unverified license, rebate, financing, or 24/7 claims
service area is above the fold
review proof uses real wording only

portalCopy.intakePrompt: Confirm the towns covered, emergency availability, repair/install services, and license text.
portalCopy.revisionLabel: Update service area or emergency copy

reviewStrategy: request reviews mentioning response speed and diagnosis clarity
summarize seasonal repair themes after launch

marginModel.notes: Higher ticket supports seasonal add-ons, but emergency claims require review.

launchChecklist: season and service area evidence captured
